message:
refactor(database): make PacketBuffer::build_packet infallible

build_packet() is a pure in-memory buffer encoder that splits a payload
into MySQL wire-protocol packets with sequence IDs.  There is no I/O,
no allocation failure path, and no validation that can fail — the
function always succeeds.  Returning Result<EncodedPacket, MySqlError>
forced every call site to propagate or unwrap an error that could never
occur.

Changes:
- Change return type from Result<EncodedPacket, MySqlError> to EncodedPacket
- Remove all .unwrap(), ?, and match Err(e) => ... at 10 call sites
  (handshake_response, auth_switch, send_query, send_query_rows,
   send_ping, disconnect, rollback, and 4 test helpers)
- The disconnect path previously swallowed build_packet errors with
  if let Ok(packet) — now unconditionally writes the COM_QUIT packet,
  which is the correct behavior since the function cannot fail

No behavioral change: every call site that previously unwrapped or
propagated now simply uses the value directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
